stories.headline: At Warning Center, Alert for the Quake, None for a Tsunami
stories.summary: A massive earthquake struck off Indonesia, generating a devastating tsunami, but experts at the Pacific Tsunami Warning Center in Honolulu had no way to warn those in its path, highlighting the limits of modern science and communication, as the US was increasing its involvement in global disaster relief efforts

stories.headline: World Business Briefing | Middle East: Israel: Military Companies Trade Stakes
stories.summary: Israeli military companies are trading stakes, reflecting the country's growing private defense industry, during a time of increased global instability

stories.headline: World Business Briefing | Asia: Japan: Sony and Samsung Sign Patent Deal
stories.summary: Sony and Samsung signed a patent deal, marking a significant partnership between two major electronics companies, as global technology competition intensified

stories.headline: If Jets and Steelers Are Mirror Images, It's Time for One of Them to Crack
stories.summary: The New York Jets are preparing to face the Pittsburgh Steelers, with the two teams being compared as mirror images, as the NFL season heats up

rounds.blurb: A massive earthquake struck off Indonesia, generating a devastating tsunami. The disaster highlighted the limits of modern science and communication in global disaster relief efforts. Israeli military companies and electronics giants like Sony and Samsung made notable business moves.

stories.headline: TENNESSEE COMPLETES SUFFRAGE VICTORY; MOVE TO RECONSIDER IS FEARED TODAY; PARTIES SPUR BATTLE FOR WOMEN'S VOTES; AMENDMENT WINS, 50 TO 46 "Antis" May Attempt to Force Fight for Rescinding it.TWO DAYS LEFT FOR ACTIONOpponents Prepare to AttackRatification on Constitutional Grounds.CONVERTED BY HIS MOTHERLegislator Whose Vote Turnedthe Scale Is Lionized bythe Suffragists.
stories.summary: The Tennessee Legislature voted to ratify the amendment granting equal suffrage to American women, making Tennessee the 36th state to approve it, as suffragists celebrated the milestone, during a time of growing women's rights movement

stories.headline: RAILWAYS GET $1,500,000,000 MORE IN RATES; 40 PER CENT. FREUGHT INCREASE IN EAST; ALL PASSENGER FARES TO GO UP 20 PER CENT.; ROADS TO EARN 6 PER CENT. Investment Is Fixed by the Commission at $18,900,000,000. WESTERN ROADS DIVIDED "Mountain Pacific Group" Is Formed--South Gets 25% Increase and West 35%.SURCHARGE ON PULLMANSThis Goes to the Roads--MilkWill Pay 20% More, as WillExcess Baggage.
stories.summary: The Interstate Commerce Commission granted railroads a significant rate increase, allowing them to earn more revenue, as the rail industry sought to recover from financial struggles, during a period of post-war economic adjustment

stories.headline: REDS FLEE BEFORE POLES, ABANDON GUNS; 30,000 CUT OFF, BREST-LITOVSK CAPTURED; SOVIET PLEDGES POLISH INDEPENDENCE; SIX RED DIVISIONS TRAPPED Capture of 30,000 to 40,000 Prisoners Expected on East Front.SEVEN DIVISIONS CRUSHEDPilsudski Moving Troops to CutOff Russians' Main Lineof Retreat.BUDENNY HELD AT LEMBERG Soviet Cavalry Leader Only 9 Miles from That City--Polish Peasants Fight with Scythes.
stories.summary: Polish forces defeated the Soviet Red Army, capturing key cities and territory, as the Polish-Soviet War intensified, with the Soviets fleeing and abandoning guns

stories.headline: REDS IN DESPERATE RALLY TO SAVE ARMY; POLES, WARNED BY WILSON, WON'T INVADE; PREMIERS BAR DEMAND TO DISARM POLAND; TRAPS ARE CLOSED ON REDS Northwest Troops Must Cut Through, Yield, or Flee Into Prussia. ESTIMATE 75,000 PRISONERS Bialystok Taken as Two Armies Under Haller and Pilsudski Strike Russian Centre. LEMBERG SAVED FROM FOE Budenny's Cavalry Retreats Toward Southeast--7,000 Reds Interned in Prussia.
stories.summary: As the Polish-Soviet War raged on, President Woodrow Wilson warned Poland against invading, while Polish forces trapped and prepared to capture or disarm the Soviet Red Army, with the Soviets launching a desperate rally to save their army

rounds.blurb: Tennessee's legislature ratified the amendment granting equal suffrage to American women. The ratification marked a significant milestone in the growing women's rights movement. Railway rates also increased significantly during this time.

stories.headline: 100,000 FILE PAST KENNEDY'S BIER; FINAL RITES IN ARLINGTON TODAY; A MILE-LONG LINE Rich, Poor, Young, Old Stand for Hours -- Heat Feels 100 100,000 File Quietly Past Kennedy's Bier in Line Extending More Than a Mile ARLINGTON RITES THIS AFTERNOON 21-Car Funeral Train Will Leave for Capital After Requiem Mass Here
stories.summary: Over 100,000 people from diverse backgrounds filed past Robert Kennedy's bier in St. Patrick's Cathedral, with his family and friends in attendance, as the nation mourned his death during a tumultuous time in American politics. The coffin was guarded by a rotating group of men and boys from all walks of life.

stories.headline: M'CARTHY FORCES SEEK TO CONTROL STATE DELEGATION; Call on Democratic Party to Give Them at Least Half of the At-Large Seats MAJOR VICTORY CLAIMED Minnesota Senator Urges Also That Platform Reflect Peace and Poor Issues McCarthy Forces, Buoyed by 'Majority' Victory, Move to Control State Delegation MINNESOTAN ASKS SAY ON PLATFORM Asserts Democratic Party's Position Should Reflect Peace and Poor Issues
stories.summary: Senator Eugene J. McCarthy's campaign team, buoyed by his primary victory, sought to control the state delegation and influence the Democratic Party's platform to reflect issues of peace and poverty. McCarthy urged the party to give his supporters at least half of the at-large seats.

stories.headline: O'DWYER BEATS NICKERSON; A SENATE UPSET Organization Is Dealt Setback -- Resnick Concedes Early O'Dwyer Upsets Nickerson in Contest for Democratic Nomination for Senate RESNICK CONCEDES IN EARLY MORNING Ellenville Man Asserts He Is 'Finished' in Politics -- Upstate Tally Slow
stories.summary: Peace candidate Paul O'Dwyer, supported by Senator Eugene J. McCarthy, won the Democratic nomination for the US Senate from New York State, with his victory attributed to the peace vote. O'Dwyer's win was seen as an upset against the organization's candidate.

stories.headline: DE GAULLE SHAKES UP HIS CABINET; LABOR CHIEFS SEEK TO END STRIKE; TANKS AT PARIS AFTER MANEUVERS; ELECTIONS JUNE 23 Tensions Are Eased as Opposition Accepts Challenge at Polls De Gaulle Shakes Up His Cabinet as Labor Union Chiefs Seek to End General Strike TANKS NEAR PARIS AFTER WAR GAMES Election Is Set for June 23 -- Opposition Parties Start to Prepare for Campaign
stories.summary: French President Charles de Gaulle reshuffled his cabinet as labor union chiefs attempted to end a general strike, with elections set for June 23. The opposition parties accepted de Gaulle's proposal for elections, easing tensions and paving the way for a campaign.

rounds.blurb: Robert Kennedy's funeral procession drew 100,000 mourners. Senator Eugene McCarthy's campaign gained momentum with a primary victory. Labor unrest eased in France as President Charles de Gaulle reshuffled his cabinet.
